root_motivation: Expose two already-stable kernel surfaces — provfs xattr file provenance and memlog pre-compaction context snapshots — as MCP tools so any agent can attribute files and retrieve context history without shelling out getfattr/memlog directly.
user_persona: Joe Yen running wintermute MCP clients (Claude Code, conduit agents) on his laptop; agents call tools/call over stdio to get file provenance or recent context snapshots during sessions.

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo build --release produces provenance-mcp binary; cargo test green; MSRV 1.85; no let-chains; sigpipe::reset() is first call in main().
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs — build succeeds; grep src/main.rs for sigpipe::reset() as first non-whitespace statement

acceptance_criteria.description: provenance-mcp serve completes initialize handshake and tools/list returns exactly two tools (file_provenance, memlog_recent) with valid JSON-Schema inputSchema.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs — drive serve via stdin/stdout pipe; assert tools array length == 2 and each has inputSchema.type == object

acceptance_criteria.description: file_provenance parses a fixtured getfattr -d output string into {path, session, ts} shape; a fixture with no prov xattrs yields session:null, ts:null and Ok (not error).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs — unit tests against two fixture strings: one with both xattrs, one empty

acceptance_criteria.description: Read-only by construction: a test asserts the only external verbs are getfattr (read) and memlog show; a guard rejects setfattr, memlog write, memlog clear appearing as command literals in backend.rs.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs — scan backend.rs source text at test time; assert forbidden verbs absent

acceptance_criteria.description: memlog_recent invokes memlog show --since <DUR> --limit <N> --format json and returns parsed array; verified against a stubbed memlog binary on PATH (offline, no real memlog needed).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs — write a stub memlog script to a tempdir, prepend to PATH, call memlog_recent, assert parsed output matches stub

acceptance_criteria.description: Missing required path on file_provenance → JSON-RPC error response, no panic, no shell call. Absent getfattr or memlog, serve still starts and affected tool returns clean ToolError (not crash).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs — call tool with missing path arg; assert error response; test serve with MEMLOG_BIN=nonexistent and GETFATTR_BIN=nonexistent, assert tools/list still works

acceptance_criteria.description: README.md documents both tools, the read-only guarantee, the provfs/memlog kernel dependency with graceful degradation notes, and an MCP client config snippet.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs — grep README.md for required sections: file_provenance, memlog_recent, read-only, provfs, MCP config

scope: Binary crate provenance-mcp with subcommand serve --memlog-bin
backend.rs: file_provenance() shells getfattr; memlog_recent() shells memlog show
tools.rs: two Tool impls with JSON-Schema inputSchema
Graceful ToolError when kernel features (provfs/memlog) are absent
non_goals: Not a write interface: no setfattr, no memlog write/clear
Not a provfs kernel module: consumes existing xattrs, does not stamp them
Not a network service: stdio MCP only
Not multi-user auth: single-user local tool
Not a general xattr browser: only user.prov.session and user.prov.ts

additional.sigpipe_reset_first: sigpipe::reset() must be the first statement in main()
additional.read_only_commands: Only getfattr and memlog show are permitted as subprocess verbs

five_whys_trace.q: Why expose provfs and memlog over MCP?
five_whys_trace.a: Agents currently shell getfattr/memlog directly — fragile, not composable with MCP tool routing.
Conduit vision routes all forensic queries through MCP so any agent can call them without knowing local paths.

five_whys_trace.q: Why provfs+memlog specifically and not a generic xattr browser?
five_whys_trace.a: These are the two stable Phase-1.5 primitives the conduit survey identified: file attribution and context history. Other xattrs are out of scope.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should kernel absence be an error or a graceful degradation?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Graceful: serve starts regardless; individual tool calls return ToolError when getfattr or memlog binary is absent.

ambiguities_resolved.question: MSRV 1.85 vs 2024 edition?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: MSRV 1.85 (matching mcp-core); use edition 2021 which 1.85 supports. No let-chains (1.87+).
